name: Doug Silkstone
title: Lead Full Stack Software Engineer
about: 15 years across engineering, product, and growth. Built and sold 3 products as solo founder. Clients have collectively raised $20M USD in early-stage funding. LLM tooling work featured in MIT's Generative AI course (50,000+ students). Author of open-source automation NPM packages. Strong track record with React/TypeScript monorepo architectures, GraphQL APIs, and production observability at scale.

highlights: Designed and built full-stack platform with React 18 functional components, strict TypeScript, and GraphQL API layer
Monorepo architecture with layered package dependencies and shared component library
Terraform for infrastructure provisioning across GCP; Sentry and custom dashboards for monitoring and alerting
Revenue dashboards with Recharts, feature flag-driven rollouts
CI/CD pipeline with BVT gates, Jest + React Testing Library coverage, ESLint/Prettier enforcement
Led greenfield in-house Game Studio for top-three social casino from zero to 1M players
Feature flag-driven rollouts across five high-stakes casino games
Real-time performance monitoring and cost-per-player tracking
Built MVP game delivery mechanism with full BVT test coverage in CI pipeline
Hired and led nine-member team across frontend and QA
Built SaaS protecting 25M+ monthly sessions with real-time monitoring and alerting
GraphQL API serving real-time analytics dashboards (Recharts). Onboarded 80+ brands
Rewrote MVP to production with strict TypeScript and RTL coverage. Commercially exited 2023
Consultancy across Contra.com, Sky, Groupon, The Motley Fool, Framer, and MIT
Component library development (MUI + Storybook) and monorepo management (Yarn workspaces, Turborepo)
Lead engineer for affiliate automation covering 10,000+ brands; 250k monthly sessions
Full Jest + RTL suite with BVT checks in deployment pipeline. Sold to PE investors 2021
Go-to-market for AR Spider Robot launched in Apple Stores globally. Hired and led cross-functional team
Built React internal tools saving 240 monthly hours, cutting SEM team overhead by 40%
